title: galium angustifolium subsp. nudicaule
intro: Galium angustifolium subsp. nudicaule, also known as sticky gentian, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the gentian family and is native to western North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of sticky gentian include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Sticky gentian can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
